activity_summary: A field operations manager assigns, reschedules, or escalates a repair visit using route, parts, and SLA constraints.
command_display: lms chat google/gemma-4-e2b --system-prompt You generate one static product UI candidate for a JudgmentKit comparison fixture. Return one valid JSON object only. Do not use Markdown fences. Do not include scripts, external assets, remote fonts, or external image URLs. Respect the context boundary described in the user prompt. --prompt <prompt> --ttl 300 --yes
notes: Gemma 4 (local LLM) output captured through lms for the JudgmentKit 3x4 model UI matrix.

parsed.summary: A field operations manager reviews a specific repair visit and uses route, parts readiness, and SLA constraints to decide whether to assign the visit, reschedule it for parts, or escalate due to SLA risk.
parsed.html:
Review Repair Visit V-2187

Customer: Mallory Chen

Plan: Priority repair | Requested SLA: SLA 4h

Parts check needed

Parts Readiness

Status: Available.

Evidence: Replacement fan motor is available at the west depot.

Route Feasibility

Status: Optimized.

Evidence: Technician Reyes is 18 minutes from the site and certified for the freezer unit.

SLA Risk

Status: High Risk.

Concern: Customer gate code is missing before dispatch, risking same-day SLA compliance.

Visit Summary

Request: Kitchen freezer repair needs dispatch before the service window closes.

Policy Note: Same-day SLA visits can be assigned when parts and site access are confirmed; missing access details return to dispatch support.
